description_long: This rifle features a modern, tactical design with a compact frame and angular lines, reminiscent of submachine guns like the KRISS Vector. The body is predominantly matte black, with a polymer lower receiver and a metal upper receiver, giving it a robust yet lightweight appearance. It has a skeletonized, folding stock attached to the rear, enhancing portability and maneuverability in urban environments. The magazine is inserted at an angle in front of the trigger guard, and the rifle is equipped with iron sights mounted on a Picatinny rail along the top. The barrel is short and ends flush with the front of the receiver, with no visible muzzle device. The overall construction suggests a focus on close-quarters use, with ergonomic features for rapid handling.
description: A compact, tactical black rifle with a skeletonized folding stock, angled magazine, and iron sights on a Picatinny rail. Its short barrel and robust build are suited for urban close-quarters scenarios.
description_view_0: The rifle is seen from the front, showing the barrel opening and the narrow profile of the stock and receiver.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the skeletonized folding stock, angled magazine, and the full length of the Picatinny rail with iron sights.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the back of the stock and the slim, vertical alignment of the rifle's body.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the folding stock, magazine, and the overall compactness of the rifle.
